title: Explicit SC/VSC and race-control enrichment via FastF1
summary: FastF1 provides lap-aligned track-status and race-control streams, and official F1 strategy analysis notes that Safety Cars materially reduce pit-stop cost.
rationale: FastF1 provides lap-aligned track-status and race-control streams, and official F1 strategy analysis notes that Safety Cars materially reduce pit-stop cost. Current features mostly infer caution state indirectly from lap-time anomalies.
implementation_hint: Merge per-race lap status features such as current flag state, status changed this lap, laps since SC/VSC started, VSC-ending flag, and recent race-control events. Interact them with compound, TyreLife, and exact laps remaining.
expected_effect: Should capture cheap-stop windows and sudden pit waves better than purely internal telemetry surrogates, especially in chaotic races.
risk: Status-to-lap alignment must be causal; using messages first seen after the lap boundary would leak. Rare caution regimes can also overfit.
